editorial.title: "당 쪼개잔 거냐" 말까지 나오는 집권 2년차 당권 갈등
editorial.body: (사설) 더불어민주당이 지방선거 패배 후 민심 회복보다 당권 싸움에 집중하며 내분이 심화되고 있다. 이재명계와 정청래계 갈등, 대통령의 당무 개입 논란 등 집권여당의 책임과 비전 부재가 지적된다. |
summary: 더불어민주당이 지방선거 패배 후 당권 싸움에만 집중하고 있다.
이재명계와 정청래계 갈등으로 내분이 심화되는 중이다.
집권여당의 책임과 비전 부재가 심각한 상황이다.
editorial.pullQuote: 집권 2년차 당권 갈등으로 민심 회복보다 당권 싸움에 집중하며 내분이 심화되고 있다.
editorial.stance: 집권여당의 내분과 책임 부재를 비판하며 민심 회복을 촉구하는 입장
editorial.titleTr: Party in turmoil as 2nd-year ruling coalition faces deepening rifts, talk of split emerges
summaryTr: The Democratic Party is focused solely on internal power struggles following its defeat in the local elections.
Internal divisions are intensifying due to conflicts between the Lee Jae-myung faction and Jung Chul-ran faction.
The ruling party faces a serious lack of accountability and vision.
editorial.bodyTr: The Democratic Party of Korea is deepening internal divisions by focusing on power struggles within the party rather than recovering public support following its defeat in the local elections. Conflicts between the Lee Jae-myung faction and Jung Chung-rae faction, along with controversy over presidential intervention in party affairs, highlight the ruling party's lack of responsibility and vision.

summaryTr: Central Labor Relations Commission decides subcontractors for cafeteria and cleaning services can negotiate directly with headquarters

Catering and cleaning companies to negotiate directly with headquarters

Endless negotiations becoming reality

The Central Labor Relations Commission has decided that catering, cleaning, and other subcontractors unrelated to production can negotiate directly with Hanwha Ocean. The Ulsan Regional Labor Relations Commission ruled that Hyundai Motor must engage in negotiations with subcontractors handling in-house cafeterias, security, and other services. editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Democratic Party Should Declare 'We'll Also Relinquish All Major Standing Committees if We Become Minority
summaryTr: Democratic Party floor leader Han Byung-do expresses intention to secure chairmanship of Legislation and Judiciary Committee in latter half of parliamentary organization
People Power Party announces it will review the number of economic-related standing committees it holds
Editorial calls for minor party to declare concession of key standing committees upon transition
editorials.bodyTr: The Democratic Party should declare it will relinquish all major standing committees if it becomes the minority party. Lee Byung-do, the Democratic Party's floor leader, stated on the 16th that regarding the composition of the National Assembly in the second half of the 22nd Congress, the Democratic Party will take the position of chair of the Judiciary Committee and will review reclaiming economic-related standing committees that had been held by the People Power Party. The People Power Party cited checks on the opposition party and balance between ruling and opposition parties as reasons.

editorials.titleTr: [Editorial] Why "Nationwide Reelection" to Prolong Leadership Crosses the Line on Public Will
summaryTr: Floor leader of the People Power Party Jang Dong-hyuk announced plans to push for re-elections in seven districts.
This marks a move to pursue nationwide re-elections citing a shortage of ballots.
Critics argue it is an attempt to extend the party leadership amid controversy over distorting public sentiment.
editorials.bodyTr: The ruling People Power Party's Rep. Jang Dong-hyuk announced plans to file electoral disputes for seven regions, including six areas—Seoul, Gyeonggi, Incheon, Ulsan, Busan, and South Jeolla and Gwangju—citing ballot shortages, plus Chungcheongbuk-do, where voter rolls were reportedly omitted. Rep. Jang said a nationwide re-election would be necessary if the disputes are upheld. This crosses the line in distorting public sentiment to extend the party leadership's tenure.

leanScore.rationale: 보수진영 국민의힘 지도부의 재선거 추진을 '민심 왜곡', '도 넘은 행동', '대표직 연명 시도'로 비판하며 보수 정당의 정치적 수단화를 강하게 지적

editorial.title: [리셋 코리아] 수출국가의 힘, 개방국가의 깊이 더해야
editorial.body: 입력 종합 24면 최창용 서울대 행정대학원 교수·리셋코리아 자문위원 한국은 좁은 내수시장, 부족한 자원, 분단의 제약을 넘어 수출로 국가 역량을 축적했다. 세계와 경쟁하며 강해졌다. 반도체와 자동차, 조선과 배터리, 바이오와 방산은 그 축적의 상징이다. 최근 국내 증시 시가총액 7000조원 성취도 제조와 수출이 만든 국가 경쟁력 위에서 가능했다. 이제는 세계로 나가는 힘을 넘어, 세계가 들어와 뿌리내릴 수 있는 깊이를 갖추어야 한다. 저출생·고령화, 지역소멸, 산업인력 부족, 글로벌 인재 경쟁이 첨예한 현실에서, 사람과 자본, 기술과 데이터가 한국 안에서 머물고 성장하며 새로운 가치를 만들어 내는 능력이 곧 한국의 미래를 좌우할 것이다. 저출생 시대, 개방은 생존 전략 인구 정책이 산업 경쟁력 좌우 세계 인재가 머무는 나라 돼야 지난달 22일 서울대 국가미래전략원 주관으로 법무부와 서울대학교가 공동 개최한 ‘2030 이민정책 미래전략’ 토론회는 바로 이 질문을 던졌다. 이름은 이민정책이지만, 본질은 단순한 출입국 관리가 아니다. 인구정책이고 산업정책이며 지역정책이고, 나아가 국가전략이다. 변화는 이미 시작됐다. 2025년 말 국내 체류 외국인은 278만 명으로 전체 인구의 5.4%에 이르렀다. 유학생은 30만 명을 넘었고, 취업자격 체류 외국인도 빠르게 늘고 있다. 그러나 숫자의 증가는 개방국가의 성숙을 보장하지 않는다. 이 점에서 ‘2030 이민정책 미래전략’은 의미 있는 방향 전환을 담고 있다. 우수인재 유치, 지역 연계형 체류 모델, 디지털·AI 기반 행정은 단순한 제도 정비가 아니다. 특히 “공무원이 일하기 편한 행정”에서 “기업인과 외국인이 일하기 편한 행정”으로 바꾸겠다는 문제의식은 중요한 출발점이다. 개방국가는 외부에서 들어온 자본·인재·기술·문화가 국내 제도와 산업, 사회 구조 속에서 생산성·혁신·정착·통합으로 전환되는 나라다. 한국은 세계화와 혁신 역량에서 높은 평가를 받고 있지만, 제도 영역과 사회적 흡수력은 아직 그 수준에 미치지 못한다. 이 차이를 설명하는 개념이 전이계수다. 전이계수는 외부의 자본·인재·데이터·문화가 유입된 뒤 그것이 실제 생산성·혁신·정착·사회통합으로 바뀌는 힘을 뜻한다. 한국의 전이계수는 제조업과 대기업 부문에서는 상대적으로 높다. 그러나 서비스업과 중소기업, 지역, 외국 인재 정착 영역에서는 병목이 뚜렷하다. 복잡한 비자체계, 높은 서비스 규제, 데이터 이동의 제약, 사회적 수용성 부족이 성장의 흐름을 막고 있다. 따라서 앞으로의 정책은 부족한 노동력을 임시로 메우는 방식에 머물러서는 안 된다. 대학과 산업, 지역과 행정이 따로 움직이는 구조도 바뀌어야 한다. 이에 더해 개방은 무질서한 문호 확대와 다르다는 점을 인식해야 한다. 최근 중앙일보 사설이 지적했듯, 난민 신청·심사·이의신청·행정소송이 장기화하면서 제도의 빈틈을 악용하는 사례가 발생하고 있다. 난민협약의 정신은 보호받아야 할 사람을 보호하는 데 있다. 개방국가는 포용을 우선해야 하지만 동시에 정교해야 한다. 선량한 이주자와 난민에게는 길을 넓히고, 제도를 악용하는 브로커와 허위 신청에는 단호해야 한다. 질서없는 개방은 지속할 수 없기 때문이다. 핵심은 균형이다. 필요한 역량에는 길을 열고, 제도의 남용에는 원칙을 세워야 한다. 지역의 수요는 반영하되 주민의 수용성을 건너뛰어서는 안 된다. 산업의 인력난은 풀어야 하지만 국내 노동시장과 임금 질서를 흔드는 방식이어서는 곤란하다. 정착의 기회는 넓히되 한국어, 법질서, 공동체 책임이라는 기준은 분명해야 한다. 유입이 많아도 정착이 약하면 한국은 경유지에 머문다. 정착은 이루어져도 성장이 약하면 사회적 비용이 커진다. 성장은 가능해도 수용이 부족하면 갈등이 축적된다. 오랫동안 한국 사회를 지탱해 온 단일민족의 서사도 새로운 국가전략의 차원에서 재구성되어야 한다. 수출국가 한국은 세계로 나가며 강해졌다. 세계의 인재와 자본, 기술과 문화가 한국 안에 들어와 머물고 성장할 수 있을 때, 한국은 비로소 더 깊은 국가가 될 것이다. 국가의 필요와 국민의 신뢰, 산업의 수요와 공동체의 질서를 함께 설계하는 전략적 개방이다. 개방의 품격은 문을 얼마나 크게 여느냐가 아니라, 들어온 역량을 한국의 성장과 공동체의 안정으로 얼마나 정교하게 바꾸어 내느냐에 달려 있다. ※ 외부 필진 기고는 본지의 편집 방향과 다를 수 있습니다. 최창용 서울대 행정대학원 교수·리셋코리아 자문위원

editorial.titleTr: Reset Korea: Export Nation Must Deepen as Open Economy
summaryTr: South Korea strengthened by exports must now deepen its competitiveness through openness.
Attracting foreign talent in an era of low birth rates is both a survival strategy and industrial policy.
Orderly opening must balance settlement, growth, and social acceptance.
editorial.bodyTr:
Korea has built up national capabilities through exports, overcoming its narrow domestic market, scarce resources, and the constraints of division. The country has grown stronger through competition with the world. Semiconductors and automobiles, shipbuilding and batteries, biotechnology and defense industry are symbols of this accumulation. The recent achievement of a 700 trillion won market capitalization on the domestic stock exchange was made possible by the national competitiveness created through manufacturing and exports. Now Korea must develop not just the strength to go out into the world, but the depth for the world to come in and take root. In a reality marked by low birth rates and aging, regional depopulation, industrial labor shortages, and fierce global competition for talent, the ability of people and capital, technology and data to remain, grow, and create new value within Korea will determine the nation's future.

In an era of low birth rates, openness is a survival strategy. Population policy determines industrial competitiveness. Korea must become a country where global talent stays.

The "2030 Immigration Policy Future Strategy" forum held on the 22nd of last month, jointly hosted by the Ministry of Justice and Seoul National University under the National Future Strategy Institute, posed precisely this question. Though named immigration policy, its essence is not simply immigration control. It is population policy, industrial policy, regional policy, and ultimately, national strategy. Change has already begun. By the end of 2025, foreign nationals residing in Korea reached 2.78 million, constituting 5.4 percent of the total population. International students exceed 300,000, and foreign nationals with employment qualifications are increasing rapidly. Yet an increase in numbers does not guarantee the maturity of an open society. In this respect, the "2030 Immigration Policy Future Strategy" contains meaningful directional change. Attracting talented individuals, regionally-connected residence models, and digital and AI-based administration are not merely institutional adjustments. Particularly important is the awareness that administration should shift from "convenient for civil servants" to "convenient for entrepreneurs and foreigners." An open society is one where capital, talent, technology, and culture from outside are transformed into productivity, innovation, settlement, and integration within domestic institutions, industries, and social structures. Korea receives high marks for globalization and innovation capacity, but institutional frameworks and social absorption capacity have not yet reached that level. The concept that explains this gap is the transfer coefficient. The transfer coefficient refers to the power through which external capital, talent, data, and culture, once introduced, are actually converted into productivity, innovation, settlement, and social integration. Korea's transfer coefficient is relatively high in manufacturing and large corporations. However, bottlenecks are evident in services, small and medium enterprises, regional areas, and foreign talent settlement sectors. Complex visa systems, high service regulations, restrictions on data movement, and insufficient social acceptance are blocking the flow of growth. Therefore, future policy must not remain confined to temporarily filling labor shortages. The structure in which universities and industry, regions and administration operate separately must also change. Furthermore, it must be recognized that openness differs from indiscriminate opening of doors. As a recent editorial in the JoongAng Ilbo pointed out, cases of exploiting loopholes in the system are occurring as refugee applications, reviews, appeals, and administrative litigation become prolonged. The spirit of the Refugee Convention lies in protecting those who deserve protection. Open societies must prioritize inclusion but simultaneously be sophisticated. They must broaden pathways for well-intentioned immigrants and refugees while remaining firm against brokers who abuse the system and fraudulent applications. Orderless openness cannot be sustained. The key is balance. Pathways must be opened for needed capabilities while principles must be established against system abuse. Regional needs must be reflected without bypassing resident acceptance. Labor shortages in industry must be solved, but not in ways that destabilize the domestic labor market and wage order. Settlement opportunities should be expanded but standards regarding Korean language, legal order, and community responsibility must be clear. If inflow is large but settlement is weak, Korea remains merely a transit country. If settlement occurs but growth is weak, social costs mount. If growth is possible but acceptance is lacking, conflicts accumulate. The narrative of a single ethnicity that has long sustained Korean society must also be reconstructed in the dimension of a new national strategy. Export-oriented Korea grew stronger by going out into the world. When the world's talent and capital, technology and culture come into Korea and remain and grow, Korea will truly become a deeper nation. This is strategic openness that designs together the nation's needs and public trust, industry's demands and community order. The quality of openness depends not on how wide doors are opened, but on how precisely the capacity that enters is transformed into Korea's growth and community stability.

editorials.stance: 무질서한 문호 개방이 아닌 전략적 개방으로 인재 정착과 사회통합을 동시에 달성해야 한다.
editorials.titleTr: Reset Korea: Export Nation Must Deepen as Open Economy
summaryTr: South Korea strengthened by exports must now deepen its competitiveness through openness.
Attracting foreign talent in an era of low birth rates is both a survival strategy and industrial policy.
Orderly opening must balance settlement, growth, and social acceptance.
editorials.bodyTr:
Korea has built up national capabilities through exports, overcoming its narrow domestic market, scarce resources, and the constraints of division. The country has grown stronger through competition with the world. Semiconductors and automobiles, shipbuilding and batteries, biotechnology and defense industry are symbols of this accumulation. The recent achievement of a 700 trillion won market capitalization on the domestic stock exchange was made possible by the national competitiveness created through manufacturing and exports. Now Korea must develop not just the strength to go out into the world, but the depth for the world to come in and take root. In a reality marked by low birth rates and aging, regional depopulation, industrial labor shortages, and fierce global competition for talent, the ability of people and capital, technology and data to remain, grow, and create new value within Korea will determine the nation's future.

In an era of low birth rates, openness is a survival strategy. Population policy determines industrial competitiveness. Korea must become a country where global talent stays.

The "2030 Immigration Policy Future Strategy" forum held on the 22nd of last month, jointly hosted by the Ministry of Justice and Seoul National University under the National Future Strategy Institute, posed precisely this question. Though named immigration policy, its essence is not simply immigration control. It is population policy, industrial policy, regional policy, and ultimately, national strategy. Change has already begun. By the end of 2025, foreign nationals residing in Korea reached 2.78 million, constituting 5.4 percent of the total population. International students exceed 300,000, and foreign nationals with employment qualifications are increasing rapidly. Yet an increase in numbers does not guarantee the maturity of an open society. In this respect, the "2030 Immigration Policy Future Strategy" contains meaningful directional change. Attracting talented individuals, regionally-connected residence models, and digital and AI-based administration are not merely institutional adjustments. Particularly important is the awareness that administration should shift from "convenient for civil servants" to "convenient for entrepreneurs and foreigners." An open society is one where capital, talent, technology, and culture from outside are transformed into productivity, innovation, settlement, and integration within domestic institutions, industries, and social structures. Korea receives high marks for globalization and innovation capacity, but institutional frameworks and social absorption capacity have not yet reached that level. The concept that explains this gap is the transfer coefficient. The transfer coefficient refers to the power through which external capital, talent, data, and culture, once introduced, are actually converted into productivity, innovation, settlement, and social integration. Korea's transfer coefficient is relatively high in manufacturing and large corporations. However, bottlenecks are evident in services, small and medium enterprises, regional areas, and foreign talent settlement sectors. Complex visa systems, high service regulations, restrictions on data movement, and insufficient social acceptance are blocking the flow of growth. Therefore, future policy must not remain confined to temporarily filling labor shortages. The structure in which universities and industry, regions and administration operate separately must also change. Furthermore, it must be recognized that openness differs from indiscriminate opening of doors. As a recent editorial in the JoongAng Ilbo pointed out, cases of exploiting loopholes in the system are occurring as refugee applications, reviews, appeals, and administrative litigation become prolonged. The spirit of the Refugee Convention lies in protecting those who deserve protection. Open societies must prioritize inclusion but simultaneously be sophisticated. They must broaden pathways for well-intentioned immigrants and refugees while remaining firm against brokers who abuse the system and fraudulent applications. Orderless openness cannot be sustained. The key is balance. Pathways must be opened for needed capabilities while principles must be established against system abuse. Regional needs must be reflected without bypassing resident acceptance. Labor shortages in industry must be solved, but not in ways that destabilize the domestic labor market and wage order. Settlement opportunities should be expanded but standards regarding Korean language, legal order, and community responsibility must be clear. If inflow is large but settlement is weak, Korea remains merely a transit country. If settlement occurs but growth is weak, social costs mount. If growth is possible but acceptance is lacking, conflicts accumulate. The narrative of a single ethnicity that has long sustained Korean society must also be reconstructed in the dimension of a new national strategy. Export-oriented Korea grew stronger by going out into the world. When the world's talent and capital, technology and culture come into Korea and remain and grow, Korea will truly become a deeper nation. This is strategic openness that designs together the nation's needs and public trust, industry's demands and community order. The quality of openness depends not on how wide doors are opened, but on how precisely the capacity that enters is transformed into Korea's growth and community stability.

editorial.title: Generation shut out
editorial.body: Young adults face shrinking job opportunities, widening wealth gap, fading hopes for future Statistics confirm the grim reality facing the younger generation. According to the Ministry of Data and Statistics' May 2026 Employment Trends report released on June 11, employment among young people ages 15-29 shrank by 255,000 from a year earlier. It was the sharpest decrease since January 2021, when the country was in the midst of the COVID-19 pandemic, and marked the 43rd consecutive month of decline. The job-market woes facing young people, who will lead our society in the future, are becoming increasingly prolonged. Despite an unprecedented export boom fueled by the semiconductor industry, job opportunities for young people are vanishing. While employees at some major corporations benefiting from the semiconductor supercycle are receiving performance bonuses in the hundreds of millions of won, many young people cannot even secure jobs with annual salaries in the tens of millions of won. This is a telling illustration of the troubling reality that employment polarization is reaching alarming levels. A June 11 report by the Bank of Korea found that the proportion of people in their 20s and 30s among households in the bottom quintile for both net assets and income nearly doubled from 7.9 percent in 2020 to 15.2 percent in 2025. During this period, the share of every other age group either held steady or declined, while that of people in their 20s and 30s rose. This is a warning sign that many young people are falling through the cracks of the labor market into the lower-income bracket without ever having had a chance to gain a proper foothold in the workforce. In the past, even those who found it difficult to get a job could still aspire to buy a home and build wealth through steady savings once they entered the workforce. However, today's young people feel helpless and discouraged in the face of stark inequalities that exist from the very start of their adult lives. The wealth gap between these young people and the older generation has widened significantly, particularly because of rising real estate prices. The median apartment price in Seoul exceeded 1.2 billion won ($792,000) this year, climbing by more than 100 million won since the start of the year. The benefits of rising real estate prices have been concentrated among middle-aged and older people who already own homes. The older generation bears significant responsibility for this. They have monopolized stable and regular jobs, entrenched a seniority-based wage system and made layoffs difficult through powerful labor unions. Companies are reluctant to hire new workers because they must shoulder the high labor costs of existing employees. It is young people who are bearing the brunt of the resulting hiring squeeze. As many as 140,000 manufacturing jobs were lost in May, marking the 23rd consecutive month of decline. If the current administration's plan to extend the mandatory retirement age to 65 is implemented, employment prospects for young people will deteriorate even further. Artificial intelligence is creating a new source of job insecurity. Employment in the professional, scientific and technical services sectors fell by 89,000 in May from a year earlier. AI is rapidly replacing tasks traditionally performed by entry-level white-collar workers. The solution begins with youth employment. The nation must focus its resources and capabilities on creating quality jobs for young people. First and foremost, companies must be encouraged to take the lead in job creation. To do so, the government must first remove regulations that discourage companies from hiring and investing, such as the Yellow Envelope Act. The rigid employment system that discourages companies from hiring should be reformed, and any extension of the mandatory retirement age should be pursued cautiously so as not to harm young people's employment prospects. No country has a future if its young people are falling into poverty. The most pressing challenge facing South Korea's economy today is to create a society in which young people can once again look to the future with hope. editorial.pullQuote: Many young people are falling through the cracks of the labor market into the lower-income bracket.
summary: Youth employment plummeted 255,000 in a year, the worst drop since COVID-19 lockdowns began.
Young adults are trapped in poverty while older generations hoard stable jobs and real estate wealth.
AI and rigid hiring rules threaten entry-level positions as the wealth gap between generations widens.
editorial.stance: Government must urgently reform labor regulations and prioritize job creation for young people or face societal collapse.
summaryTr: 청년 고용이 1년간 25만 5000명 감소했으며, 이는 코로나19 봉쇄 이후 최악의 낙폭이다.
청년들은 빈곤에 갇혀 있는 반면 기성세대는 안정적인 일자리와 부동산 자산을 독점하고 있다.
인공지능과 경직된 채용 규칙이 진입 수준의 일자리를 위협하면서 세대 간 부의 격차가 더욱 벌어지고 있다.
editorial.bodyTr:
청년들이 직업 기회 축소, 빈부격차 확대, 미래에 대한 희망 상실에 직면

통계가 젊은 세대가 직면한 암울한 현실을 보여주고 있다. 6월 11일 발표된 데이터통계부의 2026년 5월 고용동향 보고서에 따르면 15~29세 청년층 고용이 1년 전 대비 25만5000명 감소했다. 이는 국가가 코로나19 팬데믹을 겪고 있던 2021년 1월 이후 가장 큰 낙폭이며 43개월 연속 감소를 기록했다. 앞으로 우리 사회를 이끌어갈 청년층이 직면한 고용 문제가 갈수록 장기화되고 있다. 반도체 산업의 호황에 힘입어 사상 최고의 수출 붐을 누리고 있음에도 청년층의 일자리는 사라지고 있다. 반도체 슈퍼사이클의 수혜를 받는 일부 대기업 직원들이 수억 원대의 성과급을 받는 동안, 많은 청년층은 연봉 수천만 원대의 일자리도 구하지 못하고 있다. 이는 고용 양극화가 심각한 수준에 도달했다는 점을 보여주는 사례다. 한국은행이 6월 11일 발표한 보고서에 따르면 순자산과 소득 모두 최하위 20% 계층에 속한 가구 중 20·30대의 비중이 2020년 7.9%에서 2025년 15.2%로 거의 2배 증가했다. 이 기간 다른 모든 연령대의 비중은 제자리이거나 감소했으나 20·30대의 비중만 증가했다. 이는 많은 청년들이 노동시장에서 제대로 발판을 마련할 기회를 갖지 못한 채 저소득층으로 미끄러져 내려가고 있다는 경고 신호다. 과거에는 취업에 어려움을 겪었던 사람들도 한 번 직장에 들어가면 꾸준한 저축을 통해 집을 장만하고 자산을 늘릴 수 있다는 희망을 가질 수 있었다. 그러나 오늘날의 청년들은 성인 인생의 시작부터 존재하는 심각한 불평등 앞에서 무력감과 좌절감을 느끼고 있다. 이들 청년층과 기성세대 간 부의 격차는 특히 급등한 부동산 가격으로 인해 현저히 확대되었다. 올해 서울의 중위 아파트 가격이 12억 원을 넘었으며 연초 이후만 해도 1억 원 이상 올랐다. 급등한 부동산 가격의 혜택은 이미 집을 소유한 중장년층과 노년층에 집중되어 있다. 기성세대의 책임이 크다. 그들은 안정적이고 정규직 일자리를 독점해왔으며 연공서열 임금 체계를 공고히 했고 강력한 노동조합을 통해 정리해고를 어렵게 만들었다. 기업들은 기존 직원들의 높은 인건비를 감당해야 하기 때문에 신규 직원 채용을 꺼린다. 결국 이러한 채용 동결의 피해를 입는 것은 청년층이다. 5월 제조업 일자리가 14만 개 줄어 23개월 연속 감소했다. 정부의 정년 65세 연장 계획이 추진되면 청년층의 고용 전망은 더욱 악화될 것이다. 인공지능이 새로운 고용 불안의 원인이 되고 있다. 5월 전문과학기술 서비스업 고용이 1년 전 대비 8만9000명 감소했다. 인공지능은 초급 사무직 근로자들이 전통적으로 수행해온 업무를 빠르게 대체하고 있다. 해결책은 청년 고용에 초점을 맞추는 것에서 시작된다. 국가는 청년층을 위한 양질의 일자리 창출에 자원과 역량을 집중해야 한다. 무엇보다 기업들이 일자리 창출을 주도하도록 유도해야 한다. 이를 위해서는 정부가 먼저 노란봉투법 같이 기업의 채용과 투자를 저해하는 규제를 철폐해야 한다. 기업의 채용을 꺼리게 하는 경직된 고용 체계를 개혁해야 하며 정년 연장은 청년층의 고용 전망을 해치지 않도록 신중하게 추진해야 한다. 청년들이 빈곤으로 떨어지는 나라는 미래가 없다. 한국 경제가 직면한 가장 시급한 과제는 청년들이 다시 미래를 희망으로 바라볼 수 있는 사회를 만드는 것이다.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: Editorial blames labor unions and employment regulations for youth joblessness, advocates deregulation (removing Yellow Envelope Act, reforming rigid employment systems), and frames unions as having 'monopolized' jobs and created 'high labor costs' rather than addressing structural inequality or corporate responsibility.

통계가 젊은 세대가 직면한 암울한 현실을 보여주고 있다. 6월 11일 발표된 데이터통계부의 2026년 5월 고용동향 보고서에 따르면 15~29세 청년층 고용이 1년 전 대비 25만5000명 감소했다. 이는 국가가 코로나19 팬데믹을 겪고 있던 2021년 1월 이후 가장 큰 낙폭이며 43개월 연속 감소를 기록했다. 앞으로 우리 사회를 이끌어갈 청년층이 직면한 고용 문제가 갈수록 장기화되고 있다. 반도체 산업의 호황에 힘입어 사상 최고의 수출 붐을 누리고 있음에도 청년층의 일자리는 사라지고 있다. 반도체 슈퍼사이클의 수혜를 받는 일부 대기업 직원들이 수억 원대의 성과급을 받는 동안, 많은 청년층은 연봉 수천만 원대의 일자리도 구하지 못하고 있다. 이는 고용 양극화가 심각한 수준에 도달했다는 점을 보여주는 사례다. 한국은행이 6월 11일 발표한 보고서에 따르면 순자산과 소득 모두 최하위 20% 계층에 속한 가구 중 20·30대의 비중이 2020년 7.9%에서 2025년 15.2%로 거의 2배 증가했다. 이 기간 다른 모든 연령대의 비중은 제자리이거나 감소했으나 20·30대의 비중만 증가했다. 이는 많은 청년들이 노동시장에서 제대로 발판을 마련할 기회를 갖지 못한 채 저소득층으로 미끄러져 내려가고 있다는 경고 신호다. 과거에는 취업에 어려움을 겪었던 사람들도 한 번 직장에 들어가면 꾸준한 저축을 통해 집을 장만하고 자산을 늘릴 수 있다는 희망을 가질 수 있었다. 그러나 오늘날의 청년들은 성인 인생의 시작부터 존재하는 심각한 불평등 앞에서 무력감과 좌절감을 느끼고 있다. 이들 청년층과 기성세대 간 부의 격차는 특히 급등한 부동산 가격으로 인해 현저히 확대되었다. 올해 서울의 중위 아파트 가격이 12억 원을 넘었으며 연초 이후만 해도 1억 원 이상 올랐다. 급등한 부동산 가격의 혜택은 이미 집을 소유한 중장년층과 노년층에 집중되어 있다. 기성세대의 책임이 크다. 그들은 안정적이고 정규직 일자리를 독점해왔으며 연공서열 임금 체계를 공고히 했고 강력한 노동조합을 통해 정리해고를 어렵게 만들었다. 기업들은 기존 직원들의 높은 인건비를 감당해야 하기 때문에 신규 직원 채용을 꺼린다. 결국 이러한 채용 동결의 피해를 입는 것은 청년층이다. 5월 제조업 일자리가 14만 개 줄어 23개월 연속 감소했다. 정부의 정년 65세 연장 계획이 추진되면 청년층의 고용 전망은 더욱 악화될 것이다. 인공지능이 새로운 고용 불안의 원인이 되고 있다. 5월 전문과학기술 서비스업 고용이 1년 전 대비 8만9000명 감소했다. 인공지능은 초급 사무직 근로자들이 전통적으로 수행해온 업무를 빠르게 대체하고 있다. 해결책은 청년 고용에 초점을 맞추는 것에서 시작된다. 국가는 청년층을 위한 양질의 일자리 창출에 자원과 역량을 집중해야 한다. 무엇보다 기업들이 일자리 창출을 주도하도록 유도해야 한다. 이를 위해서는 정부가 먼저 노란봉투법 같이 기업의 채용과 투자를 저해하는 규제를 철폐해야 한다. 기업의 채용을 꺼리게 하는 경직된 고용 체계를 개혁해야 하며 정년 연장은 청년층의 고용 전망을 해치지 않도록 신중하게 추진해야 한다. 청년들이 빈곤으로 떨어지는 나라는 미래가 없다. 한국 경제가 직면한 가장 시급한 과제는 청년들이 다시 미래를 희망으로 바라볼 수 있는 사회를 만드는 것이다.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: 100 Days of Subcontractor Protection Bill, Prime Contractors' Delay Tactics Only Fuel Conflict
summaryTr: Yellow Envelope Act reaches 100 days in effect; subcontractor negotiation requests surge.
Fewer than 10 actual negotiations have begun due to primary contractors' delays.
Escalating conflict; substantive solutions urgently needed.
editorial.bodyTr:
The Yellow Envelope Act (amendments to Articles 2 and 3 of the Trade Union Act), which allows subcontracted workers to negotiate directly with principal contractors, marked 100 days since its implementation on the 17th. While subcontractors demanding negotiations with principal contractors have increased explosively, actual cases of principal-subcontractor negotiations beginning have fallen short of 10. According to data from the Ministry of Employment and Labor, since the Yellow Envelope Act took effect, subcontractors have filed notifications of collective action intentions exceeding 800 cases, but employers have rejected the vast majority of direct negotiation requests.

The gap between the surge in notifications and the paucity of actual negotiations reflects the current state of confusion surrounding the new law. Principal contractors are exploiting legal gray areas and ambiguities to evade their negotiating obligations, while the government's enforcement has been half-hearted. Without clear guidelines on what constitutes legitimate negotiation demands and decisive government action against violations, the Yellow Envelope Act risks becoming merely symbolic legislation that fails to deliver substantive improvements for subcontracted workers.

The government must immediately clarify implementation standards and strengthen oversight to prevent principal contractors from circumventing the law's intent. Only when subcontracted workers can actually sit at the negotiating table will the Yellow Envelope Act fulfill its true purpose of protecting vulnerable workers in Korea's subcontracting system.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Is It Normal for Protesters to Block Sports Organization Access for 12 Straight Days?
summaryTr: Protesters have blocked sports organizations from entering the Olympic Park handball gymnasium for the 12th consecutive day.
Sports associations under the Korean Sport & Olympic Committee are experiencing paralyzed operations due to inability to access their offices.
The question is raised whether it is acceptable for the protesters' illegal activities to continue.
editorials.bodyTr:
Sports organizations housed in the handball gymnasium at Seoul Olympic Park, where "vote-counting center blockade protests" are taking place, have been unable to enter their offices for 12 days, resulting in mounting damage. How much longer will we tolerate the extreme illegal actions of some protesters? Member organizations of the Korea Sports Council that have offices inside the handball gymnasium have been unable to access their facilities for 12 days, causing serious operational disruptions and financial losses. The situation has become increasingly untenable, with staff unable to conduct basic administrative work, athletes unable to prepare for competitions, and essential business operations grinding to a halt.

The right to protest is a fundamental democratic right that must be protected. However, this right cannot be absolute when it infringes upon the rights and livelihoods of others. The protesters' cause, whatever it may be, does not justify preventing innocent third parties from accessing their own workplaces and conducting their legitimate activities.

Authorities must act decisively to restore normalcy. The continued blockade represents an unacceptable violation of the law and basic civil order. While dialogue and negotiation should be pursued, there must be clear consequences for those engaged in unlawful obstruction. The affected sports organizations have already suffered considerable harm, and further delay only compounds the injustice.

A democratic society functions through the rule of law, not through the coercion of minorities by organized protest groups. Authorities have a responsibility to enforce the law impartially and protect the rights of all citizens and organizations, not just those staging demonstrations.

summaryTr: Seoul City excluded MBC from its press clipping targets after the broadcaster's report on reinforcement bar omissions.
The city explicitly discriminated against MBC in official documents, labeling it a 'media outlet with biased and distorted coverage.'
Critics point out that this retaliatory measure against critical reporting constitutes a violation of press freedom.
editorials.bodyTr: Seoul City has reportedly excluded MBC (Munhwa Broadcasting Corporation) from its internal media clipping list after the broadcaster reported on missing rebar in the GTX-A Samsung Station section. According to reports, Seoul City even wrote a note on the first page of its clipping list stating "Media outlets with biased and distorted coverage are excluded. Excluded media: MBC." MBC had reported in May on the Samsung Station section construction.

editorial.titleTr: Youth Employment at Record Low ... Labor Federations Demand Immediate Legislation on Extending Retirement Age
summaryTr: Youth employment rate plummeted 2.4 percentage points to 43.8% compared to a year ago.
The number of employed persons fell by 255,000, marking 43 consecutive months of decline.
The two major labor unions demanded immediate legislation to extend the mandatory retirement age to 65.
editorial.bodyTr: Don't the Korean Confederation of Trade Unions and the Federation of Korean Trade Unions see the sharp decline in youth employment rates? In May of this year, the employment rate for young people aged 15 to 29 dropped to 43.8%, a decline of 2.4 percentage points from a year earlier. This represents the largest monthly drop since January 2021. The number of employed persons also decreased by 255,000, marking 43 consecutive months of decline. Despite this severe employment crisis hitting young people particularly hard, the two major labor federations announced on the 16th their intention to push for an immediate law extending the mandatory retirement age to 65...

editorials.body: 민주노총과 한국노총은 청년층의 고용률 급락이 보이지도 않는가. 올해 5월 청년(15~29세) 고용률은 43.8%로 1년 전보다 2.4%포인트 추락했다. 2021년 1월 이후 가장 큰 하락폭이다. 취업자 수도 25만5000명 줄어 43개월 연속 감소했다. 이처럼 고용 한파가 유독 청년에게 집중되고 있는 상황에서 양대 노총은 16일 '65세 정년 연장 즉각 법..

editorials.titleTr: Youth Employment at Record Low ... Labor Federations Demand Immediate Legislation on Extending Retirement Age
summaryTr: Youth employment rate plummeted 2.4 percentage points to 43.8% compared to a year ago.
The number of employed persons fell by 255,000, marking 43 consecutive months of decline.
The two major labor unions demanded immediate legislation to extend the mandatory retirement age to 65.
editorials.bodyTr: Don't the Korean Confederation of Trade Unions and the Federation of Korean Trade Unions see the sharp decline in youth employment rates? In May of this year, the employment rate for young people aged 15 to 29 dropped to 43.8%, a decline of 2.4 percentage points from a year earlier. This represents the largest monthly drop since January 2021. The number of employed persons also decreased by 255,000, marking 43 consecutive months of decline. Despite this severe employment crisis hitting young people particularly hard, the two major labor federations announced on the 16th their intention to push for an immediate law extending the mandatory retirement age to 65...

editorials.body: 서울시가 재건축·재개발을 통한 주택 공급에 속도를 내기 위한 10개 법령 개정안을 정부에 건의했다. 복잡한 행정절차와 규제에 가로막혀 정체된 정비사업의 활로를 뚫자는 것인데, 가장 눈에 띄는 것은 이주비 대출 확대다. 투기과열지구로 지정된 서울 전역에서 이주비 대출은 일반 주택담보대출과 마찬가지로 LTV(주택담보대출비율)가 40%로 제한되는데, 서울시는 이..
editorials.pullQuote: 서울시는 이주비 대출의 LTV 제한 완화를 통해 정비사업의 활로를 뚫자는 입장이다.
summary: 서울시가 재건축·재개발 활성화를 위해 10개 법령 개정안을 정부에 건의했다.
이주비 대출 LTV 제한을 완화해 주민 이주 부담을 줄이자는 방안이 핵심이다.
복잡한 규제 해소로 도심 주택 공급 속도를 높이려는 고육책이다.

editorials.titleTr: Seoul City Proposes Expanded Relocation Loan Program as Bold Move to Boost Urban Housing Supply
summaryTr: Seoul City has proposed 10 amendments to laws to the government to revitalize reconstruction and redevelopment projects.
The core measure is to ease LTV restrictions on relocation loan financing to reduce the burden on residents moving.
It is a last-resort measure aimed at accelerating the supply of housing in downtown areas by eliminating complex regulations.
editorials.bodyTr: Seoul City has proposed 10 amendments to government laws to accelerate housing supply through reconstruction and redevelopment projects. The aim is to break through the gridlock in renewal projects that have stalled due to complex administrative procedures and regulations. The most notable proposal is the expansion of relocation loan programs. editorials.titleTr: Jang Dong-hyuk Files Electoral Complaints in Seoul and Five Other Districts ... Transparent Attempt to Dodge Resignation
summaryTr: People Power Party avoids resignation calls following defeat in June 3 local elections
Rep. Jang Dong-hyuk files electoral complaint citing lack of ballots
Opposition leadership draws criticism for 'gimmick' ignoring public sentiment
editorials.bodyTr: The leadership of the main opposition People Power Party is inviting criticism by attempting to evade responsibility rather than heeding the stern will of the people revealed in the June 3 local elections. Party Chairman Jang Dong-hyeok, whose resignation had been anticipated, has resorted to a legal procedure known as an election petition, citing a shortage of ballots. The People Power Party held a supreme council meeting on the 15th and filed an election petition with the National Election Commission regarding six areas including Seoul, Busan, Incheon, Gyeonggi, Ulsan, and Gwangju-South Jeolla.
